title: Module 5 handout. Presentation sprint.
subtitle: 15 min – 8-slide presentation with AI
toolsIntro: AI (structure and content): ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot. Presentation tools (format): Gamma, Canva, SlidesAI, Prezent.ai, Visme, Beautiful.ai.
toolsBullets: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot – structure and content
Gamma, Canva, etc. – paste content, get slides
structure8:
1. Problem / context
2. Why it matters now
3. Solution idea
4. How it works (simply)
5. Example / use case
6. Benefits / results
7. Risks / limits
8. What to do next (CTA)
masterPrompt: Create an 8-slide presentation structure (not full content). Topic: [Enter]. Audience: [Enter]. For each slide provide: title and 2–3 key points. Format: numbered list. Tone: clear, non-tech.
fullPromptPrinciple: META (role, goal, audience) + INPUT (topic, constraints) + OUTPUT (format) + REASONING (steps) + QUALITY (3 points max, language) + ADVANCED (temperature).
sequenceSteps: Brief – topic, audience, goal, slide count, tone
Content – generate with AI, paste into tool
Quality check – clarity, audience, structure, facts
briefDefinition: Brief is the orientation for AI: what topic, who it's for, what outcome we expect. A full brief includes: topic, audience, goal, slide count, tone/format. Without a brief AI "guesses" and gives generic output.
qualityCheckPoints: No "made up" facts? (if facts – sources or assumptions)
thresholdsExplanation: ≥70% – you understand the brief, structure and quality check; you can proceed to Module 6 (project). Below 70% – we recommend reviewing the sprint and the indicated slides again.
footerText: Prompt anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
